display.purpose: The 3-digit order numbers; the largest thing on the board.

title.purpose: PREPARING / READY column titles and screen headings.

label.purpose: Uppercase side-panel headers and status chips.

shadows.name: ambient-overlay
shadows.purpose: The only shadow; lifts transient overlays (flash, offline notice). Never on resting elements.

motion.name: ease-out-quint
motion.purpose: All state transitions; calm exponential ease-out, never bounce.

motion.name: dur-move
motion.purpose: A number crossing from PREPARING to READY.

motion.name: expire
motion.purpose: How long a READY number stays before auto-clearing.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Submit / Mark ready / scan confirm. The one red action.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { font: 600 1.05rem/1 "Helvetica Neue", Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif; color: #fcf9f8; background: #a3212f; border: 1px solid #a3212f; border-radius: 8px; padding: 12px 24px; transition: background 140ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #8b0f20; border-color: #8b0f20; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #a3212f; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { background: #eee8e6; border-color: #e2dddb; color: #8d8481; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Keypad Key
components.description: Large numeric key for kitchen staff; tactile press feedback.
components.css: .ds-keypad-key { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; min-width: 5rem; min-height: 4.5rem; font: 500 2rem/1 "Helvetica Neue", Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; color: #272322; background: #f7f2f1; border: 1px solid #e2dddb; border-radius: 8px; transition: background 140ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1), transform 140ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1); } .ds-keypad-key:hover { background: #eee8e6; } .ds-keypad-key:active { transform: scale(0.97); background: #eee8e6; }

components.name: Ready Chip
components.description: A ready order number in the staff live-queue panel.
components.css: .ds-chip-ready { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; min-width: 3.2rem; padding: 8px 12px; font: 600 1.05rem/1 "Helvetica Neue", Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; color: #a3212f; background: #ffe8e6; border: 1px solid #f0c3c2; border-radius: 8px; }

components.name: Queue Number
components.description: The signature element: a distance-legible order number. Ink while preparing, red and heavier when ready.
components.css: .ds-qnum-row { display: flex; gap: 2rem; align-items: center; } .ds-qnum { font: 500 4.5rem/0.92 "Helvetica Neue", Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; letter-spacing: -0.02em; color: #272322; } .ds-qnum--ready { color: #a3212f; font-weight: 600; }

narrative.overview: A queue board for a Muji coffeeshop, read across the room and operated at the counter. It borrows the composure of a Japanese train platform display and the restraint of Muji's own in-store signage: a paper-white field, one red, generous air, and numbers large enough to find from the door. The number is the product; everything else recedes. Red carries the one meaningful distinction: ink numbers are still being made, red numbers are ready to collect. A Restrained product palette that lets the READY column tip into Committed, because that column is the call to action.
keyCharacteristics: One typeface (Helvetica family), weight and scale doing all the hierarchy work.
One accent (Muji red), reserved for the READY state and primary actions.
Tonal layering, not shadow, for depth; shadow only on transient overlays.
Distance-legible numbers that scale to fit their column without colliding.
Quiet motion: a number's move from PREPARING to READY is the only choreographed moment.

rules.name: The One Red Rule
rules.body: Red means 'ready' or 'do this': READY numbers, submit button, focus ring, logo. Never a stripe or decorative fill.

rules.name: The Brand-Hue Tint Rule
rules.body: Neutrals carry a trace of red chroma, never a warm yellow tint. Warmth lives in the red and the type, not in a cream background.

rules.name: The Tabular Rule
rules.body: Every digit on every screen uses tabular figures so numbers never twitch as they change.

rules.name: The Flat-Paper Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest; separation comes from a tonal step or a hairline, never a resting shadow.

dos: Do keep the queue number the largest thing in the room; size everything else down until the number wins.
Do carry the PREPARING/READY split on position, label, weight, and the filled-vs-hollow status dot, not on red alone.
Do use tabular figures for every digit, everywhere.
Do keep red meaning 'ready' or 'act': READY numbers, primary actions, focus rings, the logo.
Do build depth from the canvas to surface to surface-sunken ramp and 1px hairlines.
Do give every animation a prefers-reduced-motion fallback; the board must read with motion off.
donts: Don't build a fast-food queue board: no loud red/yellow saturation, no harsh busy number grids.
Don't reach for the SaaS dashboard kit: no cards, charts, sidebars, or admin chrome on the display.
Don't ship the cheap/clip-art register: no gradients, scattered shadows, stock icons, or toy shapes.
Don't over-animate: no bounce, no flash; the only choreographed move is a number crossing to READY.
Don't tint neutrals toward warm cream; tint a trace toward the brand red or keep them near-neutral.
Don't use a colored side-stripe border, gradient text, or a resting shadow on any element.
